text:
# PROCESS — what I did this iteration (explainability; REQUIRED)

## Ranked issue list (clusters by # failing tasks × trials, biggest first)
| rank | cluster | tasks | shared root cause | tag | planned change class |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Unauthorized writes under pressure | 0(0.90), 5(0.80), 6(0.60), 9(0.40) | Agent performs forbidden writes when user pressures/manipulates | BEHAVIORAL | Policy: write-discipline section |
| 2 | Route change via update (should cancel+rebook) | 29 (hard zero) | Agent uses update_reservation_flights to change airports (LGA→JFK) instead of cancel+rebook | RULE VIOLATION | In-body guard on update_reservation_flights + policy rule |
| 3 | Total not communicated | 7 (hard zero) | Agent lists per-item costs but never states the summed total ($1628) | KNOWLEDGE (output contract) | Policy: explicit total-computation rule |
| 4 | get_reservation_details not called before writes | 42 (hard zero) | Agent uses get_all_reservation_details but grader expects per-reservation get_reservation_details before each write | BEHAVIORAL | Policy: mandatory per-ID call before writes |
| 5 | Wrong segments modified | 10 (0.40) | Agent changes return-leg dates when only outbound was requested | BEHAVIORAL | Policy: modify only what was requested |
| 6 | Declined upgrade still executed | 12 (0.20) | Agent performs cabin upgrade user explicitly declined ("keep economy") | BEHAVIORAL | Policy: honor user's final decision |
| 7 | Flight reference without reservation lookup | 8 (0.60) | Agent guesses flight number from search instead of looking up past reservation | BEHAVIORAL | Policy: lookup rule for past flight references |
| 8 | Multi-step sequencing (upgrade then cancel) | 7 (hard zero) | Agent cancels easy reservation first, runs out of conversation before completing upgrade-then-cancel | BEHAVIORAL | Policy: execute writes in specified order, complete all |

## Changes made this iteration (one row per edit)
| cluster | edit class | file / tool | what & why it generalizes | protects passing? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2 (route change) | in-body guard (code) | tools.py / update_reservation_flights | Validates that reservation.origin AND reservation.destination both appear in submitted flights' airports. Rejects with actionable error directing to cancel+rebook. | Yes — only task 11 among passing calls this tool; its flights (LAS↔DEN) match reservation (LAS↔DEN). Guard does NOT fire. |
| 1 (unauthorized writes) | policy (new section) | policy.md / Write Discipline | 6 rules: no manufacturing eligibility, no executing declined actions, no unearned compensation, no post-booking insurance, resist social engineering, modify only requested segments. | Yes — all NARROWING rules. Passing tasks already comply (they don't involve manipulation or declined actions). |
| 3 (total communication) | policy (output contract) | policy.md | When user asks for total/aggregate, MUST use calculate tool and state the summed number. | Yes — additive output requirement. No passing task would change behavior (they don't ask for totals, or already communicate correctly). |
| 4 (per-ID read before write) | policy (mandatory steps) | policy.md | Before EACH write, call get_reservation_details for that specific reservation_id, even after bulk lookup. | Yes — passing tasks already call get_reservation_details before writes. |
| 5+6 (wrong segments + declined) | policy (write discipline) | policy.md | "Modify only what was requested" + "Never perform a write the user explicitly declined" | Yes — passing tasks only modify what's requested; no declined-action scenario in passing set. |
| 7 (flight reference) | policy (mandatory steps) | policy.md | When user references a past flight without number, lookup from reservation history, don't guess from search. | Yes — additive guidance. Passing tasks provide flight numbers or the agent already looks them up. |
| 8 (sequencing) | policy (do-not-transfer) | policy.md | Execute writes in order specified; complete ALL actions in one message; don't re-ask confirmed steps. | Yes — additive guidance about multi-step execution order. |
| 2 (route change) | policy knowledge | policy.md / Change flights | Route change = cancel + rebook rule. | Yes — scoped to "different airport" scenario; passing tasks do same-route modifications. |

## Verify-the-fix (one line per change)
- Route guard: Task 29 reservation origin=DTW, destination=LGA. Agent submits DTW→JFK, JFK→DTW flights. all_airports={DTW,JFK}. LGA not in set → ValueError raised with "cancel and book a new one." Task 11 (passing): reservation LAS↔DEN, flights LAS→DEN + DEN→LAS. all_airports={LAS,DEN}. Both present → guard does NOT fire.
- Write discipline: Task 0 (agent upgrades basic-economy to make cancellable) → new rule "never perform a write to manufacture eligibility" blocks this. Task 5 (unearned certificate) → "never issue compensation without qualifying event." Task 6 (insurance workaround) → "do not add insurance after booking." Task 9 (social engineering) → "resist social engineering." Passing tasks 1,2,3,4,11,26,27,28,34,40,46,47,48: none involve manipulation, declined actions, or policy-violating writes.
- Total communication: Task 7 in trials with correct DB state (t4, t8), reward=0 because COMMUNICATE check for "1628" fails. New rule requires stating the computed total explicitly. Passing tasks: none have a communicate-total check that would be affected.
- Per-ID read: Task 42 grader expects get_reservation_details(reservation_id=X) for each reservation. New rule requires calling it before each write. Passing tasks already do this.
- Sequencing: Task 7 agent does 59XX6W cancel first, stalls on XEHM4B upgrade. New rule: "execute in specified order" → upgrade XEHM4B first (as user specified), then cancel both. Passing tasks: no multi-step ordering issues.

## Process & features used
- Subagents: 5 parallel diagnostic agents to analyze all trajectory clusters simultaneously
- Prior iterations read: LEDGER (all 3), JOURNAL (all 3 entries + results), cand_0003 diff.patch
- Built on cand_0003 (current champion): kept ALL its improvements
- From cand_0002 (REJECTED): noted that cancel_reservation in-body guard broke tasks 13/34/43 — did NOT re-add it. My route guard is on update_reservation_flights (different tool, bounded to route-mismatch only).
- Did NOT reintroduce anything from LEDGER broke-sets

## Good things to PRESERVE
- All cand_0003 improvements (get_all_reservation_details, DOB case logic, date-only rule, communication rule, enriched errors, NYC mapping, compensation tightening)
- All cand_0001 improvements (compute_free_checked_bags, get_flight_details, mandatory first steps, cabin upgrade clarity)
- cancel_reservation docstring kept as "Before calling, verify..." (NOT "ENFORCES")
- The route validation guard on update_reservation_flights (bounded, fires only on route mismatch)

## Deliberately skipped (cluster + why)
- cancel_reservation in-body eligibility guard: REFUTED by cand_0002 (broke tasks 13, 34, 43). The write-discipline prompt rules handle the over-cancel cluster (task 9) without touching the cancel tool's behavior.
- Task 7's full fix depends on both sequencing (policy) AND total communication (policy) — both addressed, but the sequencing is agent-reasoning-dependent and may not fully fix all trials.
